businessSummary.oneLiner: Undisputed AI computing leader, dominating global AI infrastructure with data center GPUs and accelerators
businessSummary.description: NVIDIA leads GPU-based AI accelerated computing with a vertically integrated ecosystem spanning data center AI chips to software platforms. At GTC 2026, unveiled Vera Rubin, Vera CPU, and the 5-layer full-stack AI strategy. FY26 revenue hit $215.9B (+65% YoY) with $120B net income. Projects $1 trillion in cumulative Blackwell and Rubin chip orders through 2027.
howTheyMakeMoney: Data Center AI Accelerators (80%+ of revenue)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Semiconductor sector avg 25x, S&P 500 avg 22x. Premium but compressing fast. PEG 0.54 at 65% growth = extreme undervaluation

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Below S&P 500 avg ~21x. World's #1 AI company trading below market average is unprecedented

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: +65% YoY growth. Data center 80%+ of revenue. Net margin 55.6%

keyMetrics.name: PEG Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Below 1.0 = undervalued vs growth. 0.54 is extreme. High-growth + low-PEG is exceedingly rare

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: P/FCF 42x. Dominant cash generation with $62.56B cash on hand

keyMetrics.name: Next Earnings
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY27 Q1 results. Full quarter of Blackwell revenue and initial Vera Rubin order visibility

growthDrivers.title: Motley Fool: '$100 Oil Is the Biggest Risk to AI Stocks' (3/29)
growthDrivers.description: Oil at $100 raises data center power costs and inflation concerns. But AI chip demand is structural, decoupled from oil. NVDA's Space-1 addresses the power problem long-term

growthDrivers.title: Motley Fool: 'Investors Are Completely Wrong About NVDA' (3/28)
growthDrivers.description: Mathematical analysis shows NVDA remains meaningfully undervalued post-GTC. Market ignoring fundamentals

growthDrivers.title: Space-1: Orbital AI Data Center with 'Real Implications' (3/29)
growthDrivers.description: Vera Rubin Space-1 orbital AI module announced. Motley Fool and Forbes call it achievable technology. Unlimited solar power transcends terrestrial energy constraints

growthDrivers.title: Forward P/E Below S&P 500, Historic Anomaly (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool flags NVDA's forward P/E dropping below S&P 500 avg as unprecedented. The AI leader trading below market average signals re-rating potential

growthDrivers.title: GTC 2026: Vera Rubin, NemoClaw Open-Source AI Agents (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: 5-layer full-stack AI strategy unveiled. Vera Rubin GPU, Vera CPU, NemoClaw open-source AI agent platform strengthen software ecosystem dominance

growthDrivers.title: $1 Trillion Cumulative AI Chip Orders Through 2027
growthDrivers.description: Blackwell and Vera Rubin combined orders projected at $1 trillion minimum through 2027. Doubled from prior $500B estimate

competitiveAdvantage.summary: CUDA ecosystem lock-in, GPU+CPU+networking+software vertical integration, annual architecture cadence, overwhelming scale economies

moats.description: 5M+ CUDA developers. Switching costs are prohibitively high, maintaining GPU market dominance
GPU, CPU (Vera), networking (Spectrum-X), software (CUDA, NIM, NemoClaw) full-stack
Hopper, Blackwell, Vera Rubin annual releases. 2-3 year tech lead over competitors
FY26 revenue $215.9B, largest in semiconductors. R&D spending overwhelms competition

competitors.name: AMD
competitors.detail: MI300X entered AI GPU market but lags in software ecosystem and scale. Data center AI share below 10%

competitors.name: Google TPU
competitors.detail: Custom AI chip strong for internal use. Limited external market influence

competitors.name: Broadcom
competitors.detail: Custom ASICs for large cloud providers. Cannot compete in the general market

recentNews.headline: Oil Breaks $100 as Houthis Fire at Israel. Data Center Power Cost Concerns Rise
recentNews.significance: Houthi missile attack adds new war front. Motley Fool calls '$100 oil the biggest risk to AI stocks.' Higher data center power costs and inflation could dampen AI investment sentiment

recentNews.headline: TipRanks: ARK/Cathie Wood Sells 213,560 NVDA Shares
recentNews.significance: ARK sold across ARKK, ARKW, ARKF over two days. TipRanks says Cathie Wood is 'losing patience' with NVDA

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: Space-1 Orbital AI Data Center Has 'Real Implications'
recentNews.significance: Vera Rubin Space-1 module analyzed as achievable technology, not science fiction. Unlimited solar power solves terrestrial energy constraints

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Investors Are Completely Wrong About NVIDIA Stock'
recentNews.significance: Forward P/E below S&P 500 called an unprecedented anomaly. NVDA mathematically undervalued post-GTC

recentNews.headline: Director Mark Stevens Sells $38.5M in Stock (3/20 Trade)
recentNews.significance: 221,682 shares at $173.68 avg. Insider selling in same week as ARK selling adds psychological pressure

analystOpinions.notableComment: 42/45 Buy, 0 Sell. ARK's 213,560-share sale hasn't changed Wall Street consensus. Motley Fool: '$100 oil is biggest AI risk but NVDA fundamentals are separate'

risks.title: Oil at $100: 'The Biggest Risk to AI Stocks'
risks.description: Motley Fool analysis: $100 oil raises data center power costs, fuels inflation, dampens AI infrastructure investment sentiment. Houthi entry could push oil even higher

risks.title: ARK Sells 213,560 Shares + Insider Selling in Same Week
risks.description: ARK sold across three funds over two days. TipRanks says Wood is 'losing patience.' Director Stevens' $38.5M sale coincides, compounding sentiment pressure

risks.title: Below Both 50-Day and 200-Day Moving Averages
risks.description: Trading below SMA50 ($170.80) and SMA200 ($173.77). First time below both since 2024

risks.title: Iran War Escalation and Macro Deterioration
risks.description: Houthi entry, oil $100+, Brent $112. Inflation reignition fears push back rate cut expectations. Worst macro environment for high-multiple tech

risks.title: China Export Controls and Smuggling Fallout
risks.description: AI chip export restrictions persist. $2.5B smuggling case could trigger tighter enforcement. Annual $15-20B market opportunity at risk

buyReasons.title: Forward P/E Below S&P 500, Unprecedented Anomaly
buyReasons.rationale: Forward P/E 20.64x below S&P avg (~21x). A 65% growing AI leader cheaper than the market average has never happened. PEG 0.54 is extreme undervaluation

buyReasons.title: Space-1: The $100 Oil Era Solution
buyReasons.rationale: While $100 oil raises data center power concerns, Space-1 orbital data centers with unlimited solar fundamentally solve the terrestrial energy constraint long-term

buyReasons.title: 42/45 Analysts Buy, 65% Upside
buyReasons.rationale: Consensus $276, high $360. Zero sells reflect unwavering Wall Street confidence in fundamentals despite oil and macro risks

buyReasons.title: $1 Trillion Order Backlog and Earnings Visibility
buyReasons.rationale: Blackwell+Vera Rubin cumulative orders at $1T. Highest 2-3 year earnings visibility in semiconductors. 5/20 earnings is the catalyst

overallOpinion: Oil broke WTI $100 as Houthis entered the war. Motley Fool warns '$100 oil is the biggest risk to AI stocks' via higher data center costs and inflation. But AI chip demand is structural and decoupled from oil prices

ARK sold 213,560 shares over two days. TipRanks says Cathie Wood is 'losing patience.' Director Stevens' $38.5M sale in the same week compounds the pressure. Yet 42/45 Wall Street Buy ratings remain unchanged

Forward P/E 20.64x is still below S&P 500 avg (21x). PEG 0.54. Space-1 orbital data center could be the long-term answer to $100 oil's power concerns. The world's #1 AI company trading below market average remains an anomaly

$167.52, RSI 41. Below SMA50 ($171) and SMA200 ($174). Oil and war will drive short-term volatility, but this is macro compression, not fundamental impairment. Dollar-cost averaging into 5/20 earnings. Buy

investmentVerdict.summary: Oil at $100 and Houthi entry amplify macro risk, but AI chip demand is structural and oil-independent. Forward P/E below S&P 500, PEG 0.54, $1T order backlog are unchanged. ARK selling hasn't moved 42/45 Buy ratings. Macro compression creates opportunity to dollar-cost average

Buy.
